expectations.text: Report file is saved as a markdown file at the specified output path
Report identifies or classifies GOLD/Barrick as a commodity producer or miner
expectations.evidence: 'Stock Type: Commodity Producer — Gold & Copper Miner'

expectations.text: Report discusses gold commodity price before or prominently alongside company analysis
expectations.evidence: '## Commodity Context (This Is 80-90% of the Story)' — prominent section before quality metrics

expectations.text: Report mentions AISC (all-in sustaining cost) or production cost/breakeven
Report explains miner leverage to underlying commodity with quantified amplification
expectations.evidence: 'A 5% move in gold (~$260) at current AISC translates to roughly a 7-8% swing in per-ounce profit. Barrick's operating leverage means the stock moves ~1.5-2x the gold price percentage change.'

expectations.text: Report contains a Recommendation section with explicit BUY, SELL, or HOLD action
Report contains Conviction level (High, Medium, or Low)
Report has a Valuation Summary section with actual numeric historical averages (not all N/A)
expectations.evidence: Valuation table columns are 'Metric | Current | Peer Avg | Context' — NO historical average column. Only peer comparisons. No 5-year historical P/E or other historical multiples provided.

expectations.text: Report discusses gold price trend or outlook as part of the thesis
expectations.evidence: Extensive gold context: $5,175/oz, +$2,200 YoY, 2024 +28%, 2025 +65%, structural drivers

expectations.text: Report has a Sources section with references
Recommendation rationale connects back to the gold commodity thesis
expectations.evidence: 'This is a leveraged bet on gold staying elevated' and dedicated bottom-line section addressing user's question

expectations.text: Report calculates explicit per-unit profit margin (spot price minus AISC in $/oz or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: 'At $5,175 gold and $1,855 midpoint AISC guidance, Barrick earns ~$3,320/oz — a 64% margin'

expectations.text: Report has a dedicated commodity price section or heading (not just mentioning gold in passing)
expectations.evidence: '## Commodity Context (This Is 80-90% of the Story)' — dedicated section with table and analysis
